macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 Suffers Fifth Straight Weekly Loss Amid Middle East Tensions
items.summary: The S&P 500 fell 2% for its fifth consecutive weekly decline, the first since 2022, driven by Middle East conflict jitters and rising oil prices to $112 per barrel for Brent crude. U.S. 10-year Treasury yields rose to 4.43% as investors question central banks' ability to combat accelerating inflation.

items.title: U.S. Inflation Surges to 3.3% on Energy Shock from Iran Conflict
items.summary: Consumer prices rose at the fastest pace in nearly two years to 3.3% in March, fueled by a global energy crisis tied to the Middle East turmoil. The spike raises concerns over persistent inflation, potentially delaying Federal Reserve rate cuts despite prior easing.

items.title: Kevin Warsh's Fed Chair Nomination Hearing Spotlights Independence Debate
items.summary: Former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh faces Senate scrutiny on Tuesday over Federal Reserve independence amid President Trump's push for lower rates and threats to oust Chair Jerome Powell. Warsh pledges commitment to price stability while navigating political pressures and a DOJ probe into the Fed's headquarters renovation.

items.title: Oil Prices Climb to $112 as Middle East Ceasefire Fails to Ease Tensions
items.summary: Brent crude hit $112 per barrel despite Iran's assurance that the Strait of Hormuz remains open during a 10-day Lebanon ceasefire. Elevated energy prices are testing central banks' inflation-fighting capacity and boosting investments in energy infrastructure amid AI power demand.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges Past $76,000 Amid Recovery and Liquidations
items.summary: Bitcoin rebounded above $76,000, trading toward $77,000, driven by strong Coinbase demand and optimism ahead of U.S.-Iran ceasefire negotiations. Crypto futures liquidations reached $197-217 million, mostly shorts, boosting market cap to $2.55 trillion.

items.title: Kelp DAO Suffers $292 Million Hack in Major DeFi Breach
items.summary: Kelp DAO lost $292 million due to a hack exploiting a single-verifier setup, stealing 116,500 rsETH. The exploit has pressured altcoins and DeFi tokens amid ongoing fallout.

items.title: Arbitrum Freezes $71 Million in ETH Linked to Kelp DAO Hacker
items.summary: Arbitrum isolated 30,765-30,766 ETH worth $71 million connected to the Kelp DAO exploit, marking a significant response to the DeFi security breach.

items.title: Rave Token Plummets 95% as Ethereum Faces Struggles
items.summary: Rave token crashed 95%, while Ethereum encountered price struggles amid broader market volatility and the Kelp DAO incident.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: ARC Raiders Expedition Window Extended and Damage Requirements Revealed
items.summary: Embark Studios extended the ARC Raiders Expedition window to April 28th, 2026, 12:00 p.m. to May 11th, 2026, 8:00 a.m., and revealed damage requirements following player criticism. The studio also won the BAFTA multiplayer game award.

items.title: ARC Raiders Devs Delay Content and Tease New Map and Skins
items.summary: ARC Raiders developers announced delays for the Riven Tide update, new in-game mail, a new map teaser, and skins amid community concerns. Embark Studios celebrated a BAFTA win for best multiplayer game.

items.title: Peter Molyneux Reacts to IGN Comments on Masters of Albion
items.summary: Fable creator Peter Molyneux reads and reacts to IGN user comments about Masters of Albion, addressing fan theories like it being a Fable reboot and grammar debates.

items.title: Discipline Priest Gets Major Playstyle Shift in WoW Patch 12.05
items.summary: World of Warcraft's The War Within Patch 12.05 reintroduces heavy shielding and bubbling builds for Discipline Priest in raids and Mythic+, with disc shields returning.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Tim Cook Steps Down as Apple CEO
items.summary: Tim Cook announced his exit after 15 years as Apple CEO, with John Ternus set to become the next CEO on September 1 and Cook transitioning to executive chairman. Johny Srouji will assume an expanded role leading Hardware Engineering.

items.title: Humble Unveils Electric Cabless Autonomous Truck
items.summary: San Francisco-based startup Humble emerged from stealth with a $24M seed round led by Eclipse, unveiling the fully electric, cabless Humble Hauler autonomous truck.

items.title: India Overtakes China in US Tech Exports
items.summary: India has become the top smartphone exporter to the US with 44% market share and $30B in exports, signaling a major shift in global supply chains.

items.title: Indo-Korea Tech Alliance Launched
items.summary: India and South Korea announced KIND-X and semiconductor hubs to pursue joint dominance in chips, space-tech, and Indo-Pacific strategy.

items.title: US Air Force Cancels RTX GPS Ground-Control Network
items.summary: The US Air Force terminated RTX's ground-control network for next-generation GPS satellites due to years of delays and cost overruns.

items.title: Meta Installs Employee Tracking Software
items.summary: Meta is deploying tracking software on US staff computers to monitor mouse movements, clicks, and keystrokes in work apps for data collection.

items.title: Senator Warren Raises Concerns Over Nvidia's SchedMD Acquisition
items.summary: US Senator Elizabeth Warren expressed worries about Nvidia's acquisition of SchedMD, which controls software for US government supercomputers, citing national security and competition risks.
